programs.title: Marooned with Ed Stafford 2
programs.desc: Former British Army captain and experienced traveler Ed Stafford continues to overcome new challenges.He became known to viewers as a steadfast man who is able to cope with any extreme situation.So far, among others:he underwent a test of survival for 60 days on one of the uninhabited islands of Fiji, where he had no supplies of food, clothing or necessary equipment.Moreover, he walked the trail leading along the Amazon River, which earned him a place in history and the Guinness Book of Records.In the next series of the program "Ed Stafford Beyond Civilization", the traveler once again sets off to the most hostile corners of the world, including the Gobi Desert in Mongolia and the crocodile-infested Guatemalan jungle.

programs.title: Shocking dishes
programs.desc: Each country has its own traditional dishes.Unusual dishes include:baked bats or bee larvae in honey.Jarosław Nowak gets to know the strangest cuisines in the world.

programs.title: Shocking dishes
programs.desc: This time, Jarosław Nowak's goal is Kazakhstan.It is a country of contrasts, where you can see wild, green steppes and, on the other hand, modern, rapidly developing cities.First, the host visits Almaty, where he tries to discover what Kazakhs consider the strangest delicacy.He sees a traditional slaughterhouse and the world's largest camel farm.His plate includes sheep's head, camel meat and horse meat, which the local population considers to be Kazakh doping.According to culinary tradition and ancient customs, no part of the dish can be wasted, and the preparation of the dish itself is a real celebration.

programs.title: The Dead Files
programs.desc: Medium Amy Allan and retired homicide detective Steve DiSchiavi team up to investigate unexplained paranormal phenomena in haunted locations across the United States.
